title: Stop extracting data: Run serverless Python natively in BigQuery!
summary: Google Cloud has made Python User Defined Functions (UDFs) generally available in BigQuery, allowing users to run custom Python code natively within SQL statements without infrastructure management. The feature supports importing libraries like BeautifulSoup and machine learning tokenizers for serverless execution.
writeup: The video covers the general availability of Python User Defined Functions in BigQuery, which let custom Python code run natively inside SQL statements without managing infrastructure. This removes the need to extract data to a separate environment for Python processing. Concrete takeaways: the UDFs execute serverlessly, support importing libraries such as BeautifulSoup, and can load machine learning tokenizers for local inference.
